batch_summary: At a wedding, Rachel and Ross encounter the newlyweds, Mindy and Barry. Rachel learns a shocking rumor: to cover up her running out on their wedding, Barry's parents told everyone she went insane from syphilis. Meanwhile, back at the apartment, Joey unsuccessfully tries to arrange a double date with his ex, and Chandler struggles with receiving messages from a married woman, expressing his frustration to Phoebe.
cumulative_summary: At a formal wedding venue, Rachel, wearing a prominent pink dress, expresses her anxiety to Ross. After receiving several cryptic remarks from guests about being "up and about," Rachel confronts the newlyweds, her ex-fiancé Barry and his new wife Mindy. She is shocked to discover that after she ran out on her own wedding to Barry, his parents covered up the embarrassment by telling everyone Rachel had gone insane from syphilis. Ross tries to humorously defuse her embarrassment. Meanwhile, the scene shifts back to the apartment, where Joey unsuccessfully tries to coordinate a double date with his ex-girlfriend Angela and her new partner. In the kitchen, Phoebe confronts Chandler about ignoring a barrage of messages on his laptop. A frustrated Chandler finally reveals the source of his distress: the woman messaging him is married.

batch_summary: Back at the apartment, Phoebe enthusiastically convinces a hesitant Chandler to meet his married online love interest in person. Meanwhile, at the wedding reception, Monica tries to convince herself she doesn't need children to appease Richard, but seeing young girls nearby makes her realize she absolutely wants them. Finally, the best man, Joel, stands up to deliver a toast, beginning with a story about Barry's first date with Rachel.

batch_summary: During the wedding toast, the best man Joel publicly embarrasses Rachel by joking about her revealing dress. To defend her, Ross stands up and delivers an awkward speech to the guests. Despite Ross's well-meaning intentions, his embarrassing defense mortifies Rachel further, prompting her to abruptly leave the reception and tell him she will meet him in the parking lot.

batch_summary: Richard promises Monica he will raise a child with her, offering to do early feedings and coach soccer just to keep her. While touched, Monica realizes that his repeated use of "if I have to" means he does not truly want another child. Heartbroken, she decides she cannot have a baby with someone who is only doing it out of obligation. They tearfully confess their love for one another and share a final, bittersweet slow dance, signaling the end of their relationship.
